sanskrit_devanagari:
तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयोः ।
गुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते ॥ २८ ॥
sanskrit_iast:
tattva-vit tu mahā-bāho
guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ |
guṇā guṇeṣu vartanta
iti matvā na sajjate || 28 ||

sanskrit_sources.agreement: exact (raw HTML scrape; akṣara sequence as published)

anvaya.meaning: the knower of the Absolute Truth

translations.verbatim_quote: One who is in knowledge of the Absolute Truth, O mighty-armed, does not engage himself in the senses and sense gratification, knowing well the differences between work in devotion and work for fruitive results.
(See holy-bhagavad-gita.org for full Mukundananda rendering; agreement with Prabhupada noted in disagreements section where it diverges materially.)

commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: The knower of the Absolute Truth is convinced of his awkward position in material association. He knows that he is part and parcel of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Kṛṣṇa, and that his position should not be in the material creation.

commentaries.translator: Swami Gambhirananda (Advaita Ashrama edition of Sankara-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.28 Tu, but, on the other hand; he who is a knower, tattva-vit, a knower of the facts;-knower of what kinds of facts?-guna-karma-vibhagayoh, about the varieties of the gunas and actions, i.e.
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Shankara: tattva-vit tu — but the knower of the truth — guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ — regarding the divisions of guna and the divisions of karma — knowing guṇāḥ guṇeṣu vartante — that the gunas operate among the gunas — na sajjate — does not become attached. The realised one sees that the senses (gunas) act on the sense-objects (also gunas), and the Self has no part in this operation. Hence no attachment. The verse names the cognitive shift that liberates without requiring abstention.

commentaries.translator: Swami Adidevananda (Sri Ramakrishna Math edition of Ramanuja's Gita-bhasya, as reproduced on bhagavad-gita.us)
commentaries.verbatim_excerpt: 3.28 But he who knows the truth about the divisions of the Gunas and their actions — namely, about the division among Sattva etc., on the one hand, and the divisions among their respective functionings on the other hand — it is he who, realising that Gunas, i.e., Sattva etc., are
commentaries.summary_paraphrase: Ramanuja: the tattva-vit knows the gunas (sattva, rajas, tamas — the modes of prakṛti) as the producers of all action. He knows karma as the effect of guna-operation. Seeing 'guṇāḥ guṇeṣu vartante' — the gunas acting on the gunas — he does not identify himself as the doer. He acts but does not get attached, because he has located doership where it actually sits: in prakṛti, not in the jīva.

disagreements_among_translators.word: guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ (the divisions of guna and karma)

positions.rendering: one who is in knowledge of the Absolute Truth understands the difference between work in devotion and work for fruitive results
the knower of the truth distinguishes between the divisions of the gunas and karma

the tattva-vit knows the divisions among the gunas (sattva-rajas-tamas) and the divisions among karma; seeing 'the gunas operate among the gunas,' he attributes action to its real source and remains unattached

the tattva-vit knows the gunas as the modes of prakṛti and karma as their effect; seeing the gunas (acting through the senses) operating on the gunas (the sense-objects also being products of prakṛti), he does not identify with the action and remains unattached

disagreements_among_translators.explanation: The verse's technical phrase guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ has been read by Shankara as the divisions WITHIN gunas and WITHIN karma (a taxonomy claim) and by Ramanuja as the divisions BETWEEN gunas and the karma they produce (a causality claim). Both readings produce the same operational result — the tattva-vit does not get attached — but the philosophy underneath is differently structured. Translators flatten both.

literal_meaning: But, O mighty-armed one, the knower of the truth (tattva-vit) regarding the divisions of guna and karma, knowing 'the gunas operate among the gunas,' does not get attached.
traditional_meaning_consensus: The complement to 3.27. The tattva-vit (knower of truth) sees the operation of the gunas — guṇāḥ guṇeṣu vartante (the gunas operate among the gunas) — and therefore does not get attached to action as if it were his own production. The verse names the cognitive shift that frees the realised actor from the binding-to-fruits while still permitting full action.
